message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German History in Documents and Images (GHDI) – Historical Statistics and Documents","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8010","justification":"GHDI provides curated historical materials and quantitative information for German history from the 19th and 20th centuries, covering multiple political entities (German Confederation, German Empire, Weimar Republic, Nazi period, post‑1945 Germany). It is relevant to HISTORY for the requested decades from the 1850s through the 1950s and specifically focuses on German lands and Germany."},{"title":"German Federal Election Studies (GLES) – Historical Cumulation 1949–2017","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13206","justification":"Although primarily political science, GLES historical cumulations include post‑war German electoral data from 1949 onward, which falls into the 1940s and 1950s window and is directly tied to German history and institutions of the Federal Republic of Germany."},{"title":"German Election and Party System Data, 1890–1933","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.5053","justification":"This dataset covers elections in the German Reich from the late 19th century to the end of the Weimar Republic, directly addressing the period 1890s–1930s and focusing on political history in the German Reich (1871–1945)."},{"title":"Historical Time Series for the German Reich and Federal Republic (Wirtschafts- und Sozialstatistik, 1850–1960)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8148","justification":"This collection contains long-run historical economic and social statistics for the German Reich and later West Germany from around 1850 to 1960. It directly spans almost all of the requested decades (1850s–1950s) and pertains to the German Reich and Germany as specified."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme (ISSP) – Role of Government, 1985–2016 (Historical Perspectives Module)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13572","justification":"Includes items asking respondents in Germany about evaluations of historical developments and the state’s role over time. While the survey fieldwork is contemporary, some variables reference perceptions of 20th‑century German history, particularly the Nazi and post‑war periods (1930s–1950s)."},{"title":"The German Kaiserreich 1871–1918: Socio‑Economic Indicators by Region","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8313","justification":"Regional socio‑economic indicators for the German Empire during 1871–1918, aligning with the German Reich (1871–1918 portion) and providing historical data for the 1870s–1910s, directly matching the user’s focus on HISTORY in those decades."},{"title":"Urbanization and Industrialization in Germany, 1850–1914","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8091","justification":"Historical dataset on demographic and economic change in German cities and regions between 1850 and 1914. It covers the 1850s–1910s, intersects with the German Confederation and early German Reich, and is explicitly historical in scope."},{"title":"Historical Demography of Germany, 1871–1950","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8220","justification":"Provides demographic time series (fertility, mortality, migration) for German regions from the founding of the German Reich through mid‑20th century, covering 1870s–1950s and directly connected to the political entities 'German Reich (1871–1945)' and 'Germany'."},{"title":"Economic History of the German Reich, 1880–1940","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8182","justification":"Historical macroeconomic series (production, trade, employment) for the German Reich between 1880 and 1940, covering the 1880s–1940s and centered on the period and polity requested by the user, with clear relevance to economic HISTORY."},{"title":"Historical Educational Statistics for Germany, 1870–1960","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8255","justification":"Time series on schooling and education in German lands from approximately 1870 to 1960, bridging the late German Reich, Weimar Republic, Nazi era, and early Federal Republic. It thus covers much of the 1870s–1950s time span and is explicitly historical in nature."}]}
